sections.paragraph: something opposite, Arab. قُبْلُ; whence, Eze 26:9, מְחִי קָֽבֳלֹו “the striking of that which is opposite,” i.e. the battering ram, for beating down walls. Other copies have קָֽבָלֹּו kobollo, which is not amiss (see קֹטֶן ); but there are grammatical reasons against קָבֳלֹּו, as given by J. H. Michaëlis and V. d. Hooght.
